channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Who Will Next Lead the United Nations?
item.description: Four candidates hope to reshape the U.N.’s future amid global fragmentation and anti-multilateral sentiment.

item.title: A dangerous blind spot in Donald Trump’s Iran war strategy
item.description: America sets too little store by foreigners’ beliefs and values. It’s a costly mistake, each time

item.title: China Doesn’t Always Win When the U.S. Loses
item.description: The Iran war exposes the limits of zero-sum thinking about great powers.

item.title: Global energy markets are on the verge of a disaster
item.description: Scenarios now range from bad to awful

item.title: 6 Things I Wish I Knew About the U.S. and Israeli Positions on Iran
item.description: Key knowledge gaps make it hard to assess how the war will end.

item.title: The stablecoin market has got too stable
item.description: Rapid growth in dollar-backed cryto has stalled

item.title: Why China’s exports will keep on rising
item.description: Our new analysis shows how the country shrugged off the effects of Donald Trump’s tariffs

item.title: Scientists are still learning from the Chernobyl nuclear disaster
item.description: Their work should make future accidents less deadly

item.title: The U.S. Has Ditched Professional Diplomacy
item.description: The first priority is Trump’s image, not national interests.

item.title: US Senate 2026 forecast
item.description: A friendly map may not be enough for Republicans to hold the Senate

item.title: US House of Representatives 2026 forecast
item.description: Democrats are heavy favourites to flip the House

item.title: Who Wants to Be an American Diplomat?
item.description: The State Department has launched a throwback recruitment campaign following layoffs and changes to diversity policies.

item.title: Lebanon’s Moment of Reckoning
item.description: How to make sure the cease-fire weakens Hezbollah instead of strengthening it.

item.title: Russia Is Making Bank on Trump’s Iran War
item.description: Pricey oil and sanctions relief mean smiles in Moscow.

item.title: Israel’s open-ended wars have eroded its security
item.description: Binyamin Netanyahu has brought about a big shift in its defence doctrine

item.title: From Allbirds to Glossier, millennial brands have lost their mojo
item.description: They have been squeezed by competitors old and new

item.title: Our midterms forecast predicts pain for Donald Trump
item.description: Just how bad could things get for the Republicans?

item.title: Why the 2026 NPT Review Conference – and Diplomacy – Must Not Fail
item.description: After two consecutive NPT RevCons failed to muster consensus, Vietnam’s U.N. ambassador – and the president-designate of the 11th Review Conference – says this year’s meeting must succeed.

item.title: The Deafening Silence on Offshore Wealth
item.description: The kind of hidden wealth that the Panama Papers exposed a decade ago fuels corruption, distorts markets, drives inequality, finances authoritarian regimes, and weakens democracies. Yet it endures—not because it is inevitable, but because powerful interests benefit from it.

item.title: China’s Liaoning Carrier Heads South: More Than a Routine Drill
item.description: The PLA is sending military signals amid the Philippines-U.S. Balikatan exercise and ahead of the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: Brother of Karkalpak Activist Detained for Petty Hooliganism
item.description: Zholdasbay Sagidullayev allegedly approached a district police department building and repeatedly shouted, "Alga, Karakalpakstan!"

item.title: Can Japan Finally Unlock Defense Technology Cooperation With India?
item.description: Amid Tokyo’s arms export shift, one massive potential market still remains untapped.

item.title: The Risky Geography of the Cloud
item.description: Iran's recent drone strikes on Gulf states mark the first time commercial data centers have been targeted in an organized fashion. Europe, especially, should reflect on the assumptions underlying digital-infrastructure investments and deployments, because attacks on data centers in the Middle East can jeopardize its own economic security.

item.title: The Real Reason Taiwan’s Defense Procurement Is Stalling
item.description: Doubts about U.S. credibility are making arms purchases harder to sustain.

item.title: Why Do Uzbek Female Labor Migrants Chose Turkiye?
item.description: Migration enables Uzbek women to renegotiate traditional gender roles and assume greater economic responsibility, even if their experiences are shaped by legal precarity and social vulnerability.

item.title: Trump’s Self-Defeating Attacks on the Fed
item.description: Instead of quietly winding down the meritless investigation of US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, the Trump administration has ramped it up, with prosecutors showing up unannounced at the Fed’s construction site. But the president's political charade will only delay the confirmation of Kevin Warsh as Powell’s successor.

item.title: The Strongman Era Has Peaked
item.description: Why a global era of authoritarian governance may be coming to an end.

item.title: The World After Trump
item.description: The international economic order that has prevailed since the end of World War II is gone for good. With the United States abdicating its leadership responsibilities, it falls to middle powers to construct a system that accommodates national preferences while safeguarding global stability.

item.title: Reevaluating ASEAN’s Economic Outlook Amid the Iran Conflict
item.description: Marcus Tantau from Templeton Research offers fresh insights into the region's economies as they reel from the impacts of the Hormuz oil crisis.

item.title: The West Is Still Getting Russia Wrong
item.description: While Vladimir Putin views Russia as a great power and civilizational counterweight to Western liberalism, he does not have a coherent plan to remake the world, let alone the capacity to do so. Because of this weakness, the Kremlin is focused not on domination, but on disruption.

item.title: The Global AI Threat Has Arrived
item.description: The ability of Anthropic’s new AI model, Claude Mythos Preview, to find vulnerabilities across major operating systems and web browsers has dangerous implications for today’s highly interconnected world. Instead of merely securing its own firms, the United States must address the problem through diplomacy, particularly with China.

item.title: ASEAN’s Rules of Origin Need a Rethink
item.description: ATIGA review and industrial strategy in an age of geopolitical risk

item.title: Modi Government Suffers Setback as Constitutional Amendment Defeated in Parliament
item.description: It clubbed an already passed amendment on women’s reservation with the controversial amendment to delimit electoral boundaries. The ploy flopped.

item.title: A Brief History of Southeast Asia’s Streaming Wars
item.description: The region offers a market with untapped potential for streaming entertainment, and one where disposable income is likely to rise in the years to come.

item.title: The Iran War Is an Expectations Game
item.description: How perceptions shape a conflict’s outcome.

item.title: A Grand Strategy of Consolidation
item.description: Trump can revitalize American power.

item.title: Venezuela Needs Regime Change
item.description: The narrow path to a democratic transition.

item.title: North Korea as It Is
item.description: The case for a cold peace.

item.title: The New Trade Order
item.description: Restoring balance to a broken global economy.

item.title: How North Korea Won
item.description: The Strange Triumph of Kim Jong Un.

item.title: How to Fight an Economic War
item.description: A field manual for a ruptured world.

item.title: Kim’s Dangerous Liaisons
item.description: Russia, China, and the growing North Korea threat.

item.title: How Myanmar’s Civil War Has Slipped Down the Global Crisis Hierarchy
item.description: The country's conflict is not forgotten, but has suffered from a perception of irrelevance compared to other unfolding crises.

item.title: Tim Cook hands Apple over to its hardware guru
item.description: John Turnus’s task is to transform the iPhone-maker for the AI era

item.title: Indonesia-U.S. Blanket Overflight Access: A Door That Others Will Push
item.description: Whether or not it is approved, Washington's request for overflight access could invite even more controversial proposals in the future.

item.title: Thailand to Accelerate Planning on ‘Land Bridge’ Project, Minister Says
Philippines, US Kick Off Largest Ever Balikatan Exercises Close to Regional Flashpoints
item.description: With the Iran war simmering, U.S. officials will seek to reassure Asian allies that they remain a reliable and committed partner.

item.title: The Dalai Lama’s Succession Battle: The Stakes for Tibetans and Beijing
item.description: The battle over the next Dalai Lama, between religious tradition and state power, is set to become one of the defining geopolitical stories of the coming decade.
